A touching discussion between Abdulhakim and Khaled from the Gaza Now team during food distribution in Gaza:

Abdulhakim (holding the box):
"Khaled... I swear my stomach hurts from hunger, and we're distributing food to people, and I haven't eaten a thing since morning."

Khaled (looking at him wearily):
"Sit over there, Abdulhakim, and take a break. Eat something, even a piece of bread."

Abdulhakim (slowly shaking his head):
"How can I eat when I see people standing in line waiting for a bite to eat, and children staring at the plate in my hand?"

Khaled (his voice breaking):
"Brother... the situation is difficult... the situation is catastrophic... If I'm the one distributing food, I'll take a small amount to quell my hunger. What can someone who doesn't have a single bite of food do?"

Abdulhakim (looking at the people around him):
"By God, you feel like you're falling short even when you're distributing food... Every day we see mothers carrying their children asking for food, and there's not enough for everyone."

Khaled (trying to smile despite his pain):
"But we have to be patient, Abdulhakim... If we don't stand with our families, who will?"

Abdulhakim (resuming the box):
"By God, Khalid, even when I'm hungry and my stomach hurts, I still feel like someone who doesn't have anything to eat is in a thousand times more difficult situation than me..."

Khaled (in a sad voice):
"It's true... Hunger is killing us all, but how can someone who doesn't even have a morsel of bread, a date, or water survive?"

Abdulhakim (trying to pull himself together):
"Let's continue distributing, Khalid... Maybe God will make these simple meals a mercy for their children, and may God grant us patience and strength."

Khaled (putting his hand on his shoulder):
"Amen, brother... May God give us the strength to carry on this service, and may we continue despite the pain and hunger."

Republic of Yemen
Ministry of Defense and Military Production
Presidency of the General Staff

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

To the heroic Mujahideen brothers in the Izz al-Din al-Qassam Brigades,
the military wing of the Islamic Resistance Movement – Hamas
Peace, mercy, and blessings of Allah be upon you...

On these blessed days, I extend to you my warmest congratulations and sincere greetings on the occasion of Eid al-Adha, praying to Almighty Allah to bring it upon you while you are in strength, pride, and steadfastness, and upon your heroic resistance with victory and empowerment. We see in your jihad a continuation of the journey of the Palestinian cause and a testimony to its justice and a source of pride for the Islamic nation. Palestine is in our hearts as it is in your rifles and in our belief and our destiny.

May Allah return to you and to our Palestinian nation Eid with goodness, victory, and pride, and to the Islamic nation with glory and unity, and to all the resistance fighters across the arenas with triumph and empowerment.

With all loyalty and sincerity...
Accept from us the highest expressions of appreciation and respect.

Major General
Mohammed Abdul Kareem al-Ghamari
Chief of the General Staff

Important Clarification from the Gaza Now Team
Based on the requests of a number of our brothers and sisters who follow us, we would like to clarify the following:
The Zionist occupation deliberately and systematically targets any humanitarian activity inside the Gaza Strip, by tracking the locations of the teams operating in the field.
We have paid a heavy price for this, as several members of the Gaza Now team were targeted merely for providing humanitarian aid. Among them are the martyrs: Hussam Shabab, Mohammed Ayash, Jihad Al-Hammami, and Mohammed Zaqout, along with other brave volunteers who shared with us the same path and mission.
In light of this painful reality, and in order to protect the families and volunteers, we have refrained for more than 4 months from publishing any photos of families or documenting the aid distribution processes.
We continue our humanitarian work in Gaza in a direct and quiet manner, without cameras or publicity—because the safety of the people is more precious than any documentation.
However, if anyone wishes to carry out a private humanitarian project or provide direct support, they may contact us, and we will fulfill their request with full honesty and care.
We apologize for the temporary absence of photos. We know that documentation is important, but lives are more valuable and sacred.
We ask Allah to help us meet your expectations and to grant us and you success in all that is good.
Gaza Now Team

The final moments of the ambulance and civil defense team before their execution in Rafah – southern Gaza Strip.

In a new crime added to the record of Zionist occupation atrocities, occupation soldiers executed 15 members of the ambulance and civil defense crews while they were performing their humanitarian duty, rescuing the wounded after an airstrike in the city of Rafah, southern Gaza Strip.

The occupation did not stop at cold-blooded murder, but went further to commit an even more heinous crime — placing the martyrs' bodies inside the ambulances, then burying them entirely in a large pit underground, in an attempt to erase the traces of the crime and conceal its evidence.

The occupation claimed that the ambulances did not bear clear markings or have their emergency lights activated. However, photos and videos that later emerged exposed these false claims, clearly showing that the vehicles were marked with ambulance and civil defense logos — confirming that the crime was deliberate.

This is a full-fledged war crime that exposes the terrorist nature of the Zionist occupation and its deliberate targeting of medical and humanitarian teams.

The Will of Journalist Hossam Shabat

"If you are reading this, it means that I have been killed—most likely targeted—by the Israeli occupation forces.

When all of this began, I was only 21 years old—a university student with dreams like anyone else. For the past eighteen months, I have dedicated every moment of my life to my people. I documented the horrors in northern Gaza minute by minute, determined to show the world the truth they tried to bury. I slept on sidewalks, in schools, in tents—wherever I could. Every day was a battle for survival. I endured hunger for months, yet I never abandoned my people.

By God, I fulfilled my duty as a journalist. I risked everything to convey the truth, and now, finally, I have found rest—something I had not known for the past eighteen months.

I did all of this out of my unwavering belief in the Palestinian cause. I believe this land is ours, and the greatest honor of my life was to die defending it and serving its people.

Now, I ask you: Do not stop talking about Gaza. Do not let the world turn away. Keep fighting, keep telling our stories—until Palestine is free.

— For the last time,
Hossam Shabat, from northern Gaza."

Clarification for Gaza Now Followers: Updates on the Ceasefire Understandings

The ceasefire understandings, mediated by Egypt, Qatar, and the United States, consist of three main phases:

1. Phase One (Partially Completed)

This phase included the exchange of prisoners, the return of some northern Gaza residents, and the entry of humanitarian aid.

While Palestinian factions have fulfilled their commitments, the Israeli occupation has not fully adhered to the agreed terms.

Despite Israel's violations, this phase is considered largely complete.



2. Phase Two (Currently Stalled)

This phase involves a prisoner exchange, where Israeli soldiers and leaders held by Palestinian factions would be released in exchange for all Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails.

This is the most challenging phase for Israel due to the scale of the exchange.

The occupation is seeking to delay this phase, refusing to withdraw from the Philadelphi Corridor and other parts of Gaza.

According to mediators (Egypt and Qatar), Netanyahu is attempting to derail this phase, and it has effectively been blocked for now.



3. Phase Three (Yet to Begin)

This phase focuses on the full reconstruction of Gaza, which depends on the implementation of Phase Two.

How Was "God's Chosen People" Used for Colonization?

1. Zionist Manipulation of the Religious Concept

Zionism (the movement that established Israel) was not originally a religious movement but a secular nationalist movement that used the idea of "divine selection" to gain support.

Zionist leaders like Theodor Herzl were not religious, yet they used religious symbolism to convince Jews to migrate to Palestine.

After occupying Palestine, Israel promoted the idea that "God promised Jews this land", even though this does not justify expelling the indigenous population.



---

2. Western Colonial Support for Israel Using "Divine Promise"

Britain supported the establishment of Israel through the Balfour Declaration (1917), using the same idea that Jews deserved to "return to their land."

The United States, especially Christian Zionist groups, promotes the belief that supporting Israel is a "religious duty," even though this is a misinterpretation of Christian teachings.

Israel continues to use this idea to gain financial and military support from Western countries.



---

3. How This Contradicts True Religious Values

Judaism itself does not call for establishing a state by force, and many Orthodox Jews (such as Neturei Karta) reject Israel because they consider it a religiously illegitimate state.

Christianity rejects racism and emphasizes that divine selection is based on faith, not ethnicity (Galatians 3:28).

Islam rejects the idea of a superior nation, affirming that superiority is based on piety, not race (Qur'an 49:13).



---

The rightful owners of Palestinian land are the Palestinians, based on historical, legal, and ethical factors:

1. Historically

Palestinian presence in the land: Palestinians have lived in this land for thousands of years, from the Canaanites to Arabs, Muslims, and Christians.

Colonialism and Israeli occupation: After the fall of the Ottoman Empire, British colonial rule took over, followed by the establishment of Israel in 1948 through the forced displacement of Palestinians (Nakba).

United Nations Resolutions: Resolutions like UN Resolution 242 and 194 call for Israel to withdraw from occupied lands and for refugees to return.

International Law: Israeli settlements are illegal, and military occupation of the West Bank, East Jerusalem, and Gaza violates the Geneva Conventions.


3. Ethically & Human Rights Perspective

Palestinians are the indigenous people who were forcibly displaced.

Israel continues to seize land by force, violating human rights.

Palestinians face apartheid-like conditions under Israeli rule.


4. What About Israelis?

Most Israelis living in Palestine today are settlers who arrived after the occupation, mainly from Europe and other regions.

Their right to live in peace does not justify taking Palestinian land or expelling its people.


Conclusion

Palestinians are the legitimate and rightful owners of the land. Israel is a colonial entity based on occupation and dispossession. A just solution would require ending the occupation and restoring Palestinian rights.

🚨 Statement issued by the Hamas movement regarding the "Tripartite statement on ceasefire negotiations":

Since the beginning of the aggression, the Islamic Resistance Movement Hamas has been keen to make the efforts of the mediating brothers in Egypt and Qatar successful, to reach a ceasefire agreement and end the war of genocide against our people, and affirmed its support for any effort to achieve a cessation of aggression.

 Hamas has engaged in several rounds of negotiations, and has provided all the necessary flexibility and positivity in order to achieve the goals and interests of our people, stop the bloodshed, and stop the genocide against them, in a way that opens the way for a prisoner exchange, relief for our people, the return of the displaced, and reconstruction of what was destroyed by the aggression. In this context, the movement agreed to the mediators’ proposal on May 6, 2024, and welcomed President Biden’s announcement on May 31, 2024, and UN Security Council Resolution 2735 in this regard, which the enemy met with rejection and continued massacres against our people. It continued to emphasize its position that it was not serious about a permanent ceasefire, and its aggressive practices against our people were practical evidence of that.  Although we and our mediating brothers in Egypt and Qatar are aware of the true intentions and positions of the occupation and its prime minister, the movement responded to the last agreement on 7/2/2024, which the enemy confronted with new conditions that were not proposed throughout the negotiation process, and went on to escalate its aggression against our people and commit more massacres, leading to the assassination of the movement's leader, the martyr leader/ Ismail Haniyeh - may God have mercy on him - in confirmation of its intentions to continue the aggression and not reach a ceasefire agreement.

Even after the announcement of the tripartite statement, the enemy committed a heinous crime and committed a massacre against the displaced people in the Al-Tabi'in School in the Al-Daraj neighborhood in Gaza while they were performing the dawn prayer on Saturday 8/10/2024, which led to the martyrdom of more than a hundred civilians and the wounding of more than 250 of them.

 In light of this, and out of concern and responsibility towards our people and their interests, the movement calls on the mediators to submit a plan to implement what they presented to the movement and approved on 7/2/2024, based on Biden’s vision and the Security Council resolution, and to oblige the occupation to do so, instead of going to more rounds of negotiations or new proposals that provide cover for the occupation’s aggression and give it more time to perpetuate the war of genocide against our people.

An overview of “Gaza Now” for Arab and Western media for “documentation and publication.”

"Gaza Now" responds to Wikipedia and all Western and American media outlets that are trying to falsify the facts and publish incorrect information about them.
"Gaza Now" is a completely independent media network, and is not affiliated with any political party.

Since its establishment in 2007, "Gaza Now News Agency" has been committed to publishing news around the clock in both Arabic and English. They also translate news from Hebrew into both Arabic and English. It is considered one of the most visited websites in the Palestinian territories, with three million monthly visitors.
As the primary source in Palestine for delivering news impartially, "Gaza Now News Agency" has become the first choice for information for a large number of Palestinians inside and outside Palestine. Its global readership is continuously growing, attracting the attention of many international news agencies regarding Palestinian affairs.
"Gaza Now News Agency" is a non-profit media organization established in 2007 with the goal of promoting independent media in Palestine, establishing relations with local, regional, and international media, and enhancing freedom of speech and media diversity as essential elements to promote democracy and human rights. "Gaza Now" is a collective entity that includes a number of independent journalists from various parts of Palestine. In addition to the news agency, "Gaza Now" is active on social media with a Facebook page that has over one and Six million followers and a Twitter account, as well as a dedicated YouTube channel , and The Gaza Now channel on Telegram, which has nearly 2 million followers, is the largest channel in the Middle East..
"Gaza Now News Agency" delivers its news reports professionally to local and global readers. It provides special features, investigative journalism, news analysis, and articles contributed by a select group of writers. The agency aims for the utmost neutrality in its news policy, facilitating information access, and promoting freedom of expression and media pluralism in Palestine. Through its English-language page, "Gaza Now News Agency" strives to provide the global audience with insights into various aspects of life in Palestine, while also providing a platform for Palestinians to address the international community.
The agency covers political, economic, cultural, and sports news from all West Bank provinces, the Gaza Strip, and the occupied areas within the Green Line, in addition to the latest developments in the Israeli context. It offers translations of excerpts from Hebrew press to present them in Arabic to its readers. It provides literary articles and distinctive investigations on a wide range of topics, from prisoner issues to projects aiming to develop the economy. To provide comprehensive coverage, "Gaza Now News Agency" maintains contact with qualified correspondents and photographers in major cities across Palestine who work in coordination with editors, translators, and other teams at the agency's main offices in Gaza City and nationwide.
In line with the public's desire to stay updated on the latest news in Palestine, "Gaza Now News Agency" sends SMS messages through social media platforms like Twitter, containing urgent news to its subscribers on their mobile phones, in both Arabic and English, covering political, social, economic, and sports news. "Gaza Now News Agency" provides this service to Palestinian mobile network users, allowing them to stay informed about the latest developments
